run_summary: Portfolio up +2.3% on mark-to-market; all four positions showing small unrealized gains. New batch is heavily weighted toward semi-cap derating (Samsung AI miss) with two defense names (AVAV re-entry, MRCY sympathy) also flagged. AVAV re-entry is blocked by re-entry discipline (conf 3, no new information beyond sector weakness already cited at exit). Semi names (ONTO, AEIS, VECO) present a tactical question: the derating looks proportional, but low-conviction RATIONAL flags without conviction-level magnitude upside do not meet the bar. MRCY and FIZZ are straightforward SKIPs. Holding all four existing positions; no new buys warranted this run.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Samsung earnings AI miss + broad semi-equipment sell-off
entries.thesis: ONTO is a metrology/inspection play with meaningful exposure to memory and advanced packaging capex, both directly tied to Samsung's spending posture. A 5%+ decline in SMH plus a Samsung AI-specific disappointment justifies the drawdown; the extended 5-day slide (down ~20%) suggests the derating is broader than one day of tape.
entries.what_kills: TSMC/SK Hynix reaffirming capex or ONTO issuing a positive pre-announcement; rebound in HBM demand commentary
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3 flagged for Samsung AI earnings miss + semi-cap sector derating. Stock is down 20.35% over 5 days; the magnitude of decline is proportional to the semiconductor industry's capex derating and ONTO's exposure to memory/advanced packaging. However, this is a RATIONAL classification with confidence 3—the move looks justified, not overdone or underdone. The flag correctly identifies the mechanism (Samsung -> memory capex cut -> ONTO pain) but does not isolate a mispricing opportunity. Waiting for either (a) a breach of support on follow-through selling (which would validate the capitulation and create a bounce setup) or (b) a clear bottom-formation signal (peer stabilization, foundry capex reaffirmation). Entry at current levels is reactive to a sector move already playing out, not a tactical contrarian call.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No specific catalyst found; group-wide defense weakness with no news thread
entries.thesis: AVAV down hard despite still being up sharply over 5 days and against a geopolitically supportive backdrop (Iran oil sanctions restored, Greenland/NATO headlines). No 8-K, no earnings, no company news — this reads as profit-taking on an extended run rather than fundamental deterioration. Fade candidate if defense tape stabilizes.
entries.what_kills: Discovery of a specific negative catalyst (contract loss, DoD budget headline hitting drone programs); if the sell-off continues on rising volume tomorrow, it's likely institutional distribution not just noise
entries.reasoning: Re-entry warning triggered: closed 11 days ago for -4.75% realized loss on restatement/officer departure overhang. New flag classifies AVAV as OVERDONE conf 3 on single-day profit-taking after multi-week rally. Re-entry discipline requires conf >= 4 AND genuinely new information. Current flag is conf 3 (fails first gate automatically—execution layer will downgrade BUY to WATCH). More critically, the new thesis ('profit-taking on extended run, no company news') does NOT constitute new information beyond the sector backdrop (defense/geopolitical strength) that was already in play at the prior exit. The thesis that drove the exit (restatement/officer departure overhang) has not been resolved, only time-decayed. Restatement scope and officer departure context should be clearer by now; if that information supports a contrarian entry, it would need conf 4+ conviction and an explicit statement of what changed in that 8-K interpretation. Holding at WATCH pending clarification or a stabilization setup.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.catalyst: Samsung earnings miss triggering semi-cap sector derating
entries.thesis: AEIS derives significant revenue from semiconductor capital equipment (power delivery for etch/deposition tools). A ~17% five-day drawdown alongside broader semi-cap weakness is consistent with sector derating. Magnitude looks appropriate given the exposure and prior valuation.
entries.what_kills: Reaffirmation of 2026 semi WFE from major foundry customers; AEIS data-center/industrial segments offsetting semi weakness
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3 flagged for semi-cap derating (Samsung AI miss -> semiconductor WFE weakness). Stock is down 17.35% over 5 days with only 0.7x volume on today's -7.43% move. The thesis is mechanically sound (AEIS derives revenue from power delivery for etch/deposition tools; Samsung miss does trigger capex cycles). However, this is a RATIONAL call, not OVERDONE/UNDERDONE—the market is pricing in a justified sector rotation. Low volume today (0.7x) suggests forced/passive selling more than fresh conviction, which could create a bounce opportunity, but current price action does not yet signal a mispricing. Waiting for a bottoming setup (support hold on volume inflection) or peer confirmation of capex cut scope before committing. The derating may have room to run if TSMC/SK Hynix reaffirm cuts; entry now is catching a falling knife, not a tactical setup.

entries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
entries.catalyst: No identified catalyst; possible customer concentration or memory capex fear
entries.thesis: VECO down while SMH +2%, TSM +4%, AVGO +3.7% is a striking divergence. Stock is down ~23% over five sessions with no 8-K and no earnings imminent — this is either late-cycle capitulation on an already-broken chart or specific customer/OEM concern (Veeco sells to memory/advanced packaging). Against this semi tape, if there's no company-specific bad news, the disconnect creates asymmetric bounce potential. Low volume (0.62x) on today's drop suggests forced/passive selling rather than fresh negative info.
entries.what_kills: An 8-K, preannouncement, or news of a major customer (Samsung, SK Hynix, TSMC) cutting orders would validate the underperformance as fundamental. Also: if ASML/AMAT commentary this week flagged advanced packaging or MOCVD weakness, VECO drop is justified.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 flagged on divergence: VECO down 23% over 5 days while SMH, TSM, AVGO rallying, with no 8-K and low volume (0.62x today). The divergence logic is sound and the thesis identifies asymmetric bounce potential. However, confidence is 3, and the absence of an 8-K or announced catalyst means we do not know the fundamental reason for the divergence. Possibilities: (1) market is repricing customer concentration (Samsung/SK Hynix memory capex) correctly and VECO is overvalued even at these levels; (2) forced/passive selling in a name with poor liquidity and no bids, which reverses once positioning stabilizes. Without a named catalyst URL or recent filing, this is a pure technical divergence trade rather than an information-driven call. Waiting for (a) a peer 8-K that clarifies memory capex context, (b) VECO stabilization on uptick in volume, or (c) a follow-up flag with more specific thesis. Current setup is interesting but not conviction-level.

entries.name: National Beverage Corp.
entries.thesis: Earnings 8-K filed 7/2 (items 2.02 + 8.01) two days after the print, stock +7.5% on 2.6x volume — normal post-earnings response for a low-float, low-coverage name. Move looks proportional; no obvious mispricing.
entries.what_kills: Reversal on discovery that the 8.01 item contained a negative disclosure (guidance withdrawal, litigation).
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3 flagged on post-earnings pop (earnings 8-K filed 7/2, stock +7.5% on 2.6x volume). The flag's own thesis is that the move looks 'normal post-earnings response for a low-float, low-coverage name' with 'no obvious mispricing.' This is a description of an efficient re-rating, not a discovery of an edge. The 8-K items (2.02 + 8.01) are boilerplate for earnings filing; volume multiple of 2.62x confirms normal participation. No new information, no identified mispricing. The flag explicitly notes the move is proportional. Skipping as this is housekeeping re-rating, not a tactical opportunity.

entries.name: Mercury Systems Inc
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst detected; likely sympathy with AVAV earnings and broader defense/ITA strength.
entries.thesis: MRCY is up double-digits with volume_multiple of only 1.07 and no 8-K, no scheduled earnings — meaning institutions are not heavily participating. The move looks like sympathy with AVAV's print plus the defense-budget narrative. Without conviction-level volume, double-digit sympathy moves in defense names tend to give back a meaningful portion within days as the news-specific tailwind to AVAV doesn't translate cleanly to MRCY's different business mix (embedded computing vs. drones).
entries.what_kills: Disclosure of an actual MRCY-specific catalyst (contract win, M&A rumor) emerges; or volume picks up materially on follow-through day signaling real institutional accumulation.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 flagged on sympathy move: MRCY up 10.99% (5-day +10.34%) with only 1.07x volume and no company-specific catalyst or 8-K. The thesis is that this is weak-hands sympathy with AVAV's print plus defense-narrative tailwind, not institutional conviction. While the mechanic is plausible (sympathy moves on light volume do fade), the setup lacks a concrete entry trigger or price target. The flag's 'what_to_learn' notes sympathy defense moves often mean-revert within a week, but that is backward-looking observation, not a forward setup. Confidence is 3, thesis lacks specificity of entry/exit. No actionable signal. Skipping.

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics Inc.
entries.catalyst: No visible catalyst; outperforming XBI meaningfully
entries.thesis: BEAM up 7%+ on above-average volume with no 8-K and no earnings scheduled. XBI is up 1.9% so some sector tailwind exists, but BEAM is outperforming the sector by 5%+ which suggests something more specific — either a data leak, an unnoticed press release, or partnership speculation. Without concrete evidence I can't articulate either bull or bear at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: Simple sector rotation with no company-specific news over the next few sessions

entries.name: Builders FirstSource
entries.catalyst: No specific catalyst; multi-day derating in housing-adjacent name
entries.thesis: BLDR down ~13% over 5 days with no 8-K or news. Could reflect rate/housing sentiment shift, tariff-linked lumber cost concerns (Trump tariff post on autos suggests continued tariff regime), or channel checks. Without a concrete thread I can't commit to a direction — the persistence of the drawdown is notable but not diagnostic.
entries.what_kills: Rate cut expectations firming and homebuilder ETF (XHB) stabilizing

entries.name: Texas Roadhouse, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown; possible read-through from Trump/Walmart beef price commentary or an unreported analyst action
entries.thesis: TXRH is down while XLY is up nearly 1%, so this is name-specific not sector. No 8-K, no earnings imminent per catalyst_signals. Trump's post about Walmart cutting ground beef prices ~15% is a potential thread — beef is TXRH's core input cost, so lower beef could actually be BULLISH for margins, not bearish. Cannot construct a confident directional read from available evidence.

entries.name: Lattice Semiconductor
entries.catalyst: New material agreement + financial obligation 8-K on 6/30, plus sector selloff
entries.thesis: LSCC filed an 8-K on 6/30 with items 1.01 (material agreement) + 2.03 (new financial obligation) — likely a debt raise or credit facility. Combined with the SMH -4.5% tape, the stock is down mid-single digits. A refinancing/new debt facility is usually not a material valuation event for a company of LSCC's size, so a portion of today's drop is likely sector-driven overshoot rather than filing-driven. Low confidence because the 1.01 could also be an acquisition financing (bearish if pricey) — the exhibit needs reading.
entries.what_kills: The 1.01 turns out to be an expensive acquisition, dilutive convert, or covenant-loosening amendment that signals stress; semi weakness broadens.

entries.name: Kratos Defense
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with AVAV earnings + broad defense strength
entries.thesis: KTOS is riding the same drone/defense narrative as AVAV, with no own 8-K. Five-day return ~20% suggests a lot has already been priced in, but with ITA +1.8% and defense as a Trump-friendly sector, momentum can persist. No clear mispricing to trade against.
entries.what_kills: Geopolitical thaw; a KTOS-specific negative headline; profit-taking after such a strong run.

entries.name: CRISPR Therapeutics
entries.catalyst: Unknown — sector strength plus unidentified company-specific driver
entries.thesis: CRSP +8% with XBI +2.5% is roughly 3x sector beta, suggesting some company-specific catalyst exists but is not in the SEC feed (could be conference presentation, analyst note, or Casgevy commercial data leak). Without identifying the driver, I cannot commit to overdone or underdone at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: n/a — need to identify catalyst first

entries.name: Align Technology, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no filings or earnings signal
entries.thesis: ALGN is up over 7% on 1.26x volume with no 8-K, no earnings signal, and XLV only up 0.5%. Cannot articulate a bull thesis at conf 3+ — could be a short-covering bounce, an unreported analyst upgrade, or GLP-1/obesity-drug crossread (Medicare obesity coverage headline today, but link to Invisalign is tenuous). Neither direction supportable from available evidence.

entries.name: Badger Meter, Inc.
entries.thesis: BMI isn't a semi or defense name, so the sector-rotation narrative doesn't apply cleanly — it's a scientific instruments / water utilities adjacent business. Volume is below average (0.84x) yet five-day move is +16%, suggesting persistent accumulation without a single news catalyst. Could be a sell-side note, a peer-derived read-through (Xylem, Roper), or M&A speculation — but with no concrete evidence, neither bull nor bear case clears the conf-3 bar.
entries.what_kills: If the 5-day move is purely momentum/short-covering with no fundamental catalyst, a quick fade is plausible — but I cannot assert that directionally without more data.
